title: Asana vs Monday.com
description: Detailed comparison of Asana's structured project management versus Monday.com's visual and flexible work OS approach.
verdict: Asana delivers robust project management with dedicated goals, portfolios, advanced reporting, and strong search capabilities that suit structured teams following formal methodologies. Monday.com offers a visually intuitive interface with faster onboarding, powerful custom automation builders, and time tracking that appeals to teams valuing flexibility and rapid setup. Marketing and creative teams that need visual templates and customizable workflows will prefer Monday.com's ease of adoption. Engineering and operations teams requiring portfolio management, workload tracking, and goals alignment will find Asana's feature depth more compelling for long-term scaling.

faqs.question: Which is better, Asana or Monday.com?
faqs.answer: Asana is better for teams that need structured project management with goals, portfolios, and robust reporting. Monday.com is better if you prefer a highly visual interface, rapid setup, and flexible automation without needing formal PM methodologies.

faqs.question: Can Asana and Monday.com be used together?
faqs.answer: Yes, through third-party integration platforms like Zapier or Make, but it is uncommon. Most teams standardize on one tool since both cover similar core functionality, and maintaining both creates duplicated work.

faqs.question: Which is more affordable, Asana or Monday.com?
faqs.answer: Asana is more affordable, especially at the free tier which supports up to 15 users with unlimited projects. Monday.com's free plan is limited to 2 users. Both charge roughly $10-11/seat/month for premium features, but Asana's base plan offers more capability per dollar.

faqs.question: Which is better for small teams?
faqs.answer: Asana is better for small teams because its generous free tier lets up to 15 members collaborate with unlimited projects and basic features. Monday.com's free plan only allows 2 users, making it hard to evaluate without paying.
